objective: Design and implement a Chrome-side element picker that captures stable target identity and feeds WebOpsForge Studio node configuration.
next_action: Reload the unpacked Chrome extension from /Users/nullin/GitHubO/WebOpsForge/apps/picker-extension and perform one manual pick on a real target page.
notes_for_resume: Picker target URL values are now references only: the Chrome extension no longer disables itself on target URL mismatch and instead enables the side panel on regular http/https pages while a Studio picker session exists. Studio publishes /api/picker/session when Pick Node is clicked; the Browser Picker panel in the 4177 UI is collapsed by default, expands on picker controls, and collapses when clicking outside or applying a pick. The extension side panel now exposes a Stop Picking button backed by STOP_PICK/PICKER_CANCELLED messages, and Open Studio focuses the most recently used existing 127.0.0.1:4177 or localhost:4177 tab before creating a new one. Node id generation preserves parent.child ids for browser steps. Action type controls are custom bilingual pickers, not native select elements; displayed labels use primary/secondary language hierarchy and persisted values remain stable English codes. Field labels have injected circular i help buttons with localized purpose/usage/source explanations. Reload the unpacked extension from apps/picker-extension and manually pick a real page element.
